mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate suppresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate and tighter financial conditions, so higher policy rates predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is above its 6-month moving average, or the sign of the return relationship flips in a rolling 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, which reduces expected earnings and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 50 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in RSAFS and HOUST signals weakening household demand and softer forward sales growth, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both fall below their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-below-MA condition does not outperform either series alone in out-of-sample sign prediction, or if 60-day returns are not negative on average in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while CPIAUCSL is cooling, real output growth outpaces inflation and margins are less pressured, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO above its 3-month average and CPIAUCSL inflation below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal is not positive in at least 55% of triggered samples, or if its 20-day return spread versus baseline is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A low inflation reading in CPIAUCSL combined with an unchanged or falling FEDFUNDS rate eases real rates and supports multiple expansion, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is below its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS is flat or down month over month
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of triggered observations, or if the combined signal fails to beat either CPIAUCSL-only or FEDFUNDS-only variants.

mechanism_narrative: Stable payroll growth in PAYEMS signals sustained income and consumption support, so higher PAYEMS levels predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS does not show a positive rolling 3-year correlation with 60-day SPX returns, or if positive-return frequency is not above 50% in the top PAYEMS growth quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 after prior strength indicates demand recession, and equities should sell off over the following quarter as earnings expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarterly print
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP declines are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of matched windows, or if the sign association is absent in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while UNRATE rises, firms face lower capacity utilization and deteriorating operating leverage, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month trend while UNRATE rises month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weak-output/rising-unemployment condition does not yield lower 20-day SPX returns than either single proxy alone, or if triggered returns are not negative on average.
